highlights.text: My general lean is ES can try to backtest this with 6854, 6878 en route.” We saw exactly this, running to 6878, then 6900+ today to end the day up at 6960.180+ points of clean, no drawdown upside.
Today saw the best long of 2026, readers were given it in advance, and it demonstrated why I only trade Failed Breakdowns.
They mean fast, vicious elevator down sells, and correspondently, equally fast squeezes.
The core I exploit I call the two siblings: The first sibling is ES suddenly goes elevator down .
This always follows the elevator down sell - never any exception in recorded history.
However, the short squeeze only occurs when ES puts in my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.

Institutions buy dips by waiting for a news driven elevator down flush/trap , waiting for a low to be taken out in the process, trapping shorts beneath the low, using that liquidity to fund their longs, and then price rips when the low recovers.
Last evening, ES sold off down to 6751 going viciously elevator down .
I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “Below there one very obvious trade is a Failed Breakdown of the December 17th 6773 low.
Trades like this are why I only trade Failed Breakdowns, and readers were able to get onboard this one.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.

post_title: Monster Squeeze For SPX. Will It Last? Feb 9th Plan
